title: The Merge Octopus
subtitle: 4 branches with order dependencies, conflicts, and a bug to revert

briefing.narrative:
Integration day. Four branches. But this time, it's not just about resolving conflicts. It's about ORDER.

Branch A changes the database setting. Branch D DEPENDS on A's database change — if you merge D before A, you get a wrong config. Branch B changes the same setting as A — conflict guaranteed. Branch C has two commits: one good, one that introduces a bug.

The Head Monk draws the dependency map:
- A must go FIRST (D depends on it)
- D goes SECOND (clean merge after A)
- B goes THIRD (conflicts with A on the database setting, must be resolved)
- C goes LAST (then you must REVERT C's buggy commit)

"Merge in the wrong order and you'll create unnecessary conflicts. Miss the bug in C and it ships to production. This is why integration is an art, not a recipe."

The cat has four tentacles. Wait, no, it's wearing four scarves. Close enough.
briefing.concept:
Complex integration requires understanding dependencies between branches:

1. Some branches depend on others being merged first
2. Merge order affects which conflicts arise
3. After merging, you may need to revert specific buggy commits

Strategy: identify dependencies, plan the merge order, resolve conflicts, then clean up bugs with git revert.
objectives: Merge the 4 branches into main in the correct order
Resolve the conflict between branch-a and branch-b
After merging branch-c, revert its buggy commit
Final config.txt must have correct values from all teams
The bug file must be reverted

bug: infinite loop in search

database: sqlite

hints.text: Merge order matters: branch-a first (database upgrade), branch-d second (depends on being after a), branch-b third (conflicts with a's database), branch-c last (then revert its bug).

1) git merge branch-a. 2) git merge branch-d (resolve: keep postgres + structured). 3) git merge branch-b (resolve: keep postgres + redis). 4) git merge branch-c (resolve: keep all). 5) git revert HEAD~1 --no-edit (reverts the bug commit from C).
